batches.title: Mexican-Spiced Chicken + Roasted Peppers & Chickpeas (Monday Lunch)
steps: 2. In a bowl, combine 450 g chicken breast (whole pieces) with 2 tbsp olive oil, 1½ tsp cumin, 1½ tsp paprika, 1 tsp garlic powder, juice of 1 lime, pinch of salt. Mix well and leave to marinate 15 min while oven heats.
3. Slice 300 g bell peppers into strips; halve 200 g cherry tomatoes. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp oregano, salt and pepper on a large roasting tray.
4. Place marinated chicken pieces on the same tray or a separate one. Roast at 200°C for 22–25 min until chicken reaches 75°C internal temperature and veg are golden.
5. Remove from oven; rest chicken 5 min, then slice into strips. Let cool completely.
6. Drain and rinse 2 × 400 g cans of chickpeas; store in a separate sealed container.

7. Store sliced chicken in one airtight container and roasted peppers/tomatoes in another. Refrigerate immediately. Use within 3 days (Monday only — this batch is consumed Monday lunch only due to portion size).
yields: ~450 g sliced cooked chicken (both portions — Monday lunch only)
~500 g roasted bell peppers and cherry tomatoes

day_of_assembly.Monday: Lunch — Mexican Chicken & Chickpea Bowl: reheat sliced chicken and roasted peppers in a pan 3–4 min, add chickpeas for last 2 min, serve over fresh couscous with lime and salad greens.

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Asian Sesame Chicken (Tuesday–Wednesday Lunches)
steps: 1. Slice 600 g chicken breast into thin strips (about 5 mm thick).
2. In a bowl, mix 3 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp ginger powder, 1 tsp garlic powder. Add chicken strips and toss to coat. Marinate 10 min.
3. Heat a wok or large non-stick pan over high heat. Working in two batches to avoid steaming, stir-fry chicken strips for 5–6 min total until golden and cooked through. Do not overcrowd the pan.
4. Spread cooked chicken on a tray to cool quickly (no more than 2 hours at room temp).
5. Parboil 200 g broccoli florets in boiling salted water for 2 min; drain, refresh under cold running water, drain again.
6. Julienne or coin-slice 200 g carrots; store raw in a sealed zip-lock bag.
7. Store cooked chicken in a sealed container and parboiled broccoli in a separate container. Refrigerate immediately. Use within 3 days (Tue and Wed = days 1 and 2 — within safe window).

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Lunch — Asian Sesame Chicken Bowl with Broccoli, Carrots & Brown Rice: stir-fry carrot coins and parboiled broccoli in sesame oil, add pre-cooked chicken, toss with soy sauce, serve over freshly cooked brown rice.
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Lunch — Asian Sesame Chicken Bowl with Zucchini, Spinach & Brown Rice: sauté sliced zucchini and spinach in sesame oil, add pre-cooked chicken, toss with soy sauce and garlic powder, serve over freshly cooked brown rice.

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Canned Lentils + White Beans — Drained & Portioned (Tue Dinner, Fri Lunch)
steps: 1. Open 2 × 400 g cans of white beans; drain and rinse thoroughly in a colander under cold water.
2. Open 2 × 400 g cans of lentils; drain and rinse in the same colander.
3. Store drained white beans in one labelled airtight container.
4. Store drained lentils in a second labelled airtight container.
5. Refrigerate both. White beans used Tuesday dinner (day 1 — well within 5-day window). Lentils used Friday lunch (day 5 — right at the edge of 5-day window; safe for legumes).
yields: ~700 g drained white beans (ready to use)

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Dinner — Tuna & White Bean Salad: combine drained white beans with canned tuna, sliced cucumber, cherry tomatoes and salad greens; dress with olive oil and white wine vinegar; serve with toasted sourdough.
day_of_assembly.Friday: Lunch — Tuna Niçoise-Style Bowl: combine drained lentils with canned tuna, sliced cucumber, cherry tomatoes and salad greens; dress with olive oil and white wine vinegar; serve with toasted sourdough and a piece of fruit.

storage.safety_note: Cooked beans and lentils keep well. Freeze in portions for quick meals.
